title: Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park
tagline: Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park — wildlife reserve of Telangana

heroImage.alt: Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park, Telangana

overview.short: Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park is a national park located in Jubilee Hills and Banjara Hills in Hyderabad, Telangana, India.
overview.description: Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park is a national park located in Jubilee Hills and Banjara Hills in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. The park has an approximate area of 390 acres (1.6 km2). The palace complex was given to Prince Mukarram Jah on his coronation by his father Prince Azam Jah in 1967. It was declared as a National park by the Andhra Pradesh state government after getting approval from the central government in the year 1998.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sri Peddamma Thalli Temple
topPlaces.description: Sri Peddamma Thalli Temple is a Hindu temple situated in the Jubilee Hills area of Hyderabad, Telangana. Dedicated to Goddess Peddamma Thalli, who is worshipped as a form of the Divine Mother, the temple is one of the well-known places of worship in the city.

topPlaces.name: Park Hyatt Hyderabad
topPlaces.description: Park Hyatt Hyderabad is a luxury hotel located in the Banjara Hills neighbourhood of Hyderabad, India that opened on 29 April 2012. Built on an area of 32,256 square metres (347,200 sq ft) the Hotel is the first urban Park Hyatt in India and 29th hotel in the Park Hyatt portfolio.

topPlaces.name: Telangana Integrated Command and Control Centre
topPlaces.description: Telangana Integrated Command and Control Centre (TGICCC) is a modern policing and emergency management facility located in Banjara Hills, Hyderabad, Telangana. It serves as the central hub for the Telangana Police, integrating technological and operational functions to enhance surveillance, crime control, and emergency response.

image.alt: Telangana Integrated Command and Control Centre

topPlaces.name: Shaikpet Mosque and Sarai
topPlaces.description: The Shaikpet Mosque and Sarai is a former mosque and a sarai (rest house) in a partial ruinous state, located near Golconda in Hyderabad, in the Hyderabad District of the state of Telangana, India. The former mosque and serai are listed as a state protected monument.

topPlaces.name: Shaikpet
topPlaces.description: Shaikpet is located near Tolichowki in Hyderabad, India. It is a mandal in Hyderabad District and one of the oldest suburbs of Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: State Gallery of Art, Chitramayee
topPlaces.description: The State Gallery of Art, also known as Chitramayee and as the State Gallery of Fine Arts, is a public fine arts gallery located in Madhapur, Hyderabad, in the state of Telangana, India. It was established in 2004.

topPlaces.name: Madhapur metro station
topPlaces.description: Madhapur Metro Station is located on the Blue Line of the Hyderabad Metro India.

topPlaces.name: Kotla Vijay Bhaskar Reddy Indoor Stadium
topPlaces.description: Kotla Vijay Bhaskar Reddy Indoor Stadium is an indoor sporting arena located in Yousufguda, Hyderabad in the Indian state of Telangana. Kotla Vijaya Bhaskara Reddy (16 August 1920 – 27 September 2001) served as the chief minister of the Indian state of United Andhra Pradesh in 1983 and then from 1992 to 1994.

image.alt: Kotla Vijay Bhaskar Reddy Indoor Stadium

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kasu Brahmananda Reddy National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sri Peddamma Thalli Temple, Park Hyatt Hyderabad, Telangana Integrated Command and Control Centre, Shaikpet Mosque and Sarai, Shaikpet.
